title: Budget waste causality model wave 1
purpose: Create a defensible model for deciding when a festival has budget-waste risk, without equating high budget with waste.
core_rule: Budget waste requires a mismatch between public cost, public value, local economic capture, satisfaction, safety/traffic externalities and contract accountability. Total budget alone is not enough.

official_data_sources.use: baseline cost, revenue and net-cost evidence
separate direct operation, subsidy and facility-heavy spending

risk_flags.description: High net public cost but weak visitor, satisfaction or local-spend evidence.
risk_flags.evidence_needed: Local Finance cost, visitor methodology, satisfaction/evaluation report.

risk_flags.description: Large economic-effect claim without transparent assumptions, duplicate-visitor control or merchant-level validation.
risk_flags.evidence_needed: Economic-effect model, card-sales by zone/industry, merchant survey.

risk_flags.description: Large outsourced package but no clear scope for safety, price, booth, traffic or satisfaction outcomes.
risk_flags.evidence_needed: RFP, task order, contract, evaluation result, settlement.

risk_flags.description: Festival shifts traffic, noise, safety, cleanup or resident inconvenience costs outside the official budget result.
risk_flags.evidence_needed: Traffic plan, civil complaints, cleanup cost, police/fire deployment, resident survey.

risk_flags.description: Public budget draws visitors, but outside vendors/night markets capture sales or cause reputation damage without being controlled.
risk_flags.evidence_needed: Booth lists, seller rules, price table, complaint log, local merchant participation.

case_links.budget_question: Do headline visitor/economic-effect claims match merchant-level and traffic-inconvenience evidence?
Did governance/operator transition and programming criticism create value-for-money risk?
Did public festival spending generate visitor trust while uncontrolled sellers damaged satisfaction?

bands.100: high-confidence waste finding only with official cost, outcome and accountability evidence
